---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---


```{python}
import os
import re
import sys
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import anndata as ad
from scipy.stats import median_abs_deviation
import decoupler as dc
```

```{python}
print(sys.executable)
```

```{python}
flex_adata = sc.read_h5ad("flex_adata_clustered.h5ad")
```

```{python}
sc.pl.umap(flex_adata, color = "COL3A1")
```

```{python}
sc.pl.violin(flex_adata, keys = "COL3A1", groupby = "poms_id", layer = "X")
```

### pseudobulk samples
```{python}
pdata = dc.pp.pseudobulk(
    adata=flex_adata,
    sample_col="poms_id",
    groups_col="celltypist_label_foetal_lung_celltypist",
    mode="sum",
    layer="counts",
    skip_checks=True
)
```

### remove samples with low cell counts
### ~ symbold inverses (so allows me to remove these samples rather than keep)
```{python}
samples = ["POMS_089", "POMS_020", "POMS_039", "POMS_048"]
pdata = pdata[~pdata.obs["poms_id"].isin(samples)].copy()
```

### select celltypes i want to run DEA on
### not only does celltypist predict a lot of celltypes, i only actually want to focus on the AT subsets for now
```{python}
subsets = ["AT1", "AT2"]
pdata = pdata[pdata.obs["celltypist_label_foetal_lung_celltypist"].isin(subsets)].copy()
```

### here we view and then remove samples where they have fewer than 10 cells or 1000 counts
### in this case poms_025 has 0 cells in the AT2 cluster, so no point having them in the comparison as it might break it
```{python}
dc.pl.filter_samples(
    adata=pdata,
    groupby=["gestation_bin", "poms_id", "celltypist_label_foetal_lung_celltypist"],
    min_cells=10,
    min_counts=1000,
    figsize=(5, 8),
)
```

```{python}
dc.pp.filter_samples(pdata, min_cells=10, min_counts=1000)
```

```{python}

```
